problem.title: Search a 2D Matrix II
problem.description: Write an efficient algorithm that searches for a value target in an m x n integer matrix where integers in each row are sorted left to right and integers in each column are sorted top to bottom.
assumptions: Rows sorted ascending, columns sorted ascending.
First element NOT necessarily > last of previous row.

example.explanation: Start top-right: if > target go left, if < target go down. O(m+n).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Search from Corner
solutions.description: Start at the top-right corner: if the current value equals the target, return true; if it is greater, move left (eliminating the column); if it is smaller, move down (eliminating the row). Each step eliminates an entire row or column, giving O(m+n) total comparisons. The key insight is that the top-right corner is the unique position where moving in one direction strictly increases values and moving in the other strictly decreases them.
